steps.title: Pinpoint what supports the claim
steps.content: The student asserts that Quispe's photographs have “ethnographic value” because they captured “diverse elements” of Peruvian society while representing subjects with “dignity and authenticity.” To support this claim, evidence must show he documented different social groups through careful, respectful documentation.

steps.title: Match the evidence to the requirement
steps.content:
The correct answer directly supports the claim by showing Quispe photographed both wealthy Peruvians and Indigenous communities while carefully documenting their peoples, sites, and customs—the essence of ethnographic work.

The other choices address unrelated factors: technical skill, international recognition, and subject popularity.

That confirms A is correct.
